asia.title: US Justice Department investigating whether Harvard aid programmes are discriminatory
asia.summary: The US Justice Department said on Monday it launched an investigation reviewing whether some Harvard University financial aid programmes were discriminatory, marking the latest fight against the university by Donald Trump’s administration. “The Justice ‌Department’s Civil Rights Division announced today it is opening a compliance review into Harvard University to determine whether its China-based financial aid programmes and practices exclude American-citizen students,” the department said in a.

asia.title: Restaurants call for more reforms to lower food prices
asia.summary: The Thai Restaurant Association is asking the government to connect raw material wholesalers with restaurants to lower ingredient costs, rather than providing subsidies for each dish.

asia.title: US seeks to limit key materials from China, others in defence supply chains
asia.summary: China is the world's biggest producer and refiner of rare earths, which are critical for high-tech products ranging from electric vehicles to missile guidance systems.

asia.title: Brazil widens lead in China’s soybean market as US shipments fall despite Trump-Xi deal
asia.summary: China imported 12.08 million tonnes of Brazilian soybeans in June, up 13.7 per cent from a year earlier, while volumes from the United States fell 20.6 per cent to 1.27 million tonnes, far off the pace implied by the purchasing deal agreed by Presidents Donald Trump and Xi Jinping, according to figures released on Monday by China’s General Administration of Customs. Total arrivals of the oilseed reached 13.55 million tonnes, a record for the month, lifted by Brazilian supply and by the clearing.

asia.title: Sihasak hails PM's 'historic' China visit
asia.summary: Foreign Affairs Minister Sihasak Phuangketkeow has described Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul's official visit to China as a historic success.

asia.title: How a national security threat label has reignited Indonesia's LGBTQ debate
asia.summary: Indonesia last October classified the "spread of LGBTQ culture" as one of the country's non-military threats. A series of developments thrust the provision into the public eye this month, sparking a debate.

asia.title: Trump imposes 50% tariffs on Canadian goods, citing disputes over cars, alcohol and cheese
asia.summary: US President Donald Trump on Monday imposed 50 per cent tariffs on most Canadian goods, declaring that Canada has unfairly discriminated against American cars, alcohol and dairy products. The move could unleash a new wave of economic chaos, with risks of higher inflation and further fraying of relations between two nations that had been closely woven together before Trump’s return to the White House. The administration official previewing the action said that Canada was one of the only nations..

asia.title: Trump order targets China-linked military mineral supply chains
asia.summary: US President Donald Trump on Monday signed an executive order aimed at reducing dependence on China for defence-related critical minerals, directing military contractors to shift away from Beijing-linked supply chains beginning in January 2027. Without naming China, Trump’s executive order stresses the need to maintain US military dominance “in an era of renewed great power competition”. “The United States must secure its supply chains against physical, cyber, and economic subversion,” it...

briefing: Global markets are bracing for potential escalation in trade tensions as President Trump imposes a 50% tariff on most Canadian goods, while the US death toll in the Iran war rises to 17, prompting fears of further conflict. In Singapore, Metro is set to close its Paragon and Causeway Point department stores as part of a retail overhaul, shifting away from traditional large department store formats. Meanwhile, the resignation of Faishal has sparked speculation about a potential Cabinet reshuffle, with analysts noting it reaffirms the PAP's high standards, and the Straits Times reports on Singaporeans from different racial backgrounds forging friendships through dance and community activities.

digests.singapore: Singapore is witnessing a blend of community building and political developments, with locals from different racial backgrounds forging friendships through dance and community activities, while the ruling People's Action Party is navigating a potential Cabinet reshuffle following the resignation of a minister. The country's retail landscape is also undergoing changes, as seen in Metro's decision to close department stores and shift away from traditional large formats. Meanwhile, the government is strengthening regulations on personal data usage, mandating organisations to inform consumers if their data is used to train AI models, amidst other international and local political developments.
digests.us: The US government is addressing various security and diplomatic concerns, including an investigation into a threat at a World Cup fan fest event and a rising death toll in the Iran war, which may lead to further escalation. Additionally, the administration is imposing tariffs on Canadian goods and dealing with the aftermath of an explosive incident at a federal building in New York City. Meanwhile, the Pentagon has confirmed nearly 100 service members have been injured since the US resumed strikes against Iran, and a court ruling has temporarily halted the Paramount-Warner Bros merger.
digests.asia: The US is taking steps to reduce its dependence on China, with the Justice Department investigating Harvard University's financial aid programmes for potential discrimination against American citizens and President Trump signing an executive order to limit China's involvement in defence supply chains. Trade tensions are also escalating, with the US imposing tariffs on Canadian goods and China increasing its imports of Brazilian soybeans while reducing those from the US. Meanwhile, in Southeast Asia, Thailand is seeking to lower food prices through reforms and Indonesia is debating the classification of LGBTQ culture as a national security threat.
digests.others: Trade tensions between the US and Canada have escalated with the imposition of 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods, reportedly in response to Canada's retaliation against previous US tariffs. Meanwhile, in the Middle East, Yemen's Houthis have announced a maritime embargo on Saudi Arabia, while the US has launched fresh strikes on Iran, warning of retaliation for the deaths of American soldiers. Protests and unrest have also been reported in Italy, Albania, and elsewhere, sparked by incidents such as the death of a Moroccan-born man in Italian police custody and controversy over resort projects in Albania.
digests.tech: The technology and innovation landscape is witnessing significant developments, including a major copyright infringement lawsuit filed by Sony Music Entertainment against Udio's AI music generator, and the FCC's plans to retroactively ban disguised DJI gadgets. Meanwhile, the investment world is also seeing notable changes, such as the inclusion of SpaceX in index funds, which raises questions about the risks and implications of such a move. Additionally, the US Space Force is seeking to purchase up to $30 billion in rocket launches, highlighting the growing importance of space exploration and technology.
digests.economist: The Economist's analysis highlights the complexities of global business and economic trends, including the misattribution of rising food prices to supermarket greed and the emergence of mysterious Chinese billionaires. Meanwhile, the publication also explores regional challenges, such as India's efforts to navigate energy shocks and China's struggles with demographic problems, including gender antagonism. Additionally, the articles touch on issues of regulation and innovation, including the dominance of SK Hynix in advanced memory chips and the bureaucratic hurdles facing America's nightlife industry.
